info.title: AgentGuard Policy Engine API
info.description:
**AgentGuard** is a runtime security platform for AI agents. It provides policy-based evaluation,
audit trails, PII detection, MCP server governance, agent hierarchy management, and OWASP compliance reporting.

## Authentication

AgentGuard uses three authentication schemes:

- **API Key** (`x-api-key` header): Your tenant API key, prefixed `ag_live_`. Used for most authenticated endpoints.
- **Agent Key** (`x-api-key` header): Agent-scoped key, prefixed `ag_agent_`. Used for evaluate calls from specific agents.
- **Admin Key** (`x-admin-key` header or `x-api-key`): Platform admin key. Required for admin-only endpoints.

## Rate Limits

- Unauthenticated: 10 requests/min per IP
- Authenticated: 100 requests/min per IP
- Signup: 5 per hour per IP

## Base URL

Production: `https://api.agentguard.tech/api/v1`

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Account creation, login, and API key management

tags.name: PII
tags.description: Personally Identifiable Information detection and redaction

tags.name: Agent Hierarchy
tags.description: A2A multi-agent policy propagation and child agent management

tags.name: Playground
tags.description: Interactive policy testing without full auth

tags.name: Alerts
tags.description: Anomaly detection alerts and custom alerting rules

tags.name: Dashboard
tags.description: Real-time evaluation statistics and activity feed

tags.name: SIEM
tags.description: SIEM export integrations (Splunk HEC, Azure Sentinel)

tags.name: Team
tags.description: Team member management with RBAC roles

tags.name: SCIM
tags.description: SCIM 2.0 user/group provisioning for IdP sync

tags.name: Pricing
tags.description: Public pricing tiers and feature limits

tags.name: Policy Git
tags.description: GitOps policy management via webhook sync

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Tenant API key (prefixed `ag_live_`) or agent key (prefixed `ag_agent_`)

AdminKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
AdminKeyAuth.description: Platform admin key (set via ADMIN_KEY environment variable)

matchedRuleId.description: ID of the policy rule that matched

piiDetected.description: Whether PII was detected in the request params

apiKey.description: Agent API key (only returned on creation)

policyScope.description: List of tools this agent is scoped to

active.description: Whether the agent is currently active

declaredTools.description: Tools declared by this agent during validation

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of this entry (chained with previous)

entitiesFound.description: Total number of PII entities found

redactedContent.description: Input content with PII replaced by [REDACTED_TYPE] placeholders

acsUrl.description: ACS callback URL to configure in your IdP

entityServiceUrl.description: Entity/audience URL to configure in your IdP

get.description: Returns API and database health status. No authentication required.

Returns a step-by-step guide any AI agent can follow to integrate AgentGuard.
No authentication required. This is the zero-friction onboarding path: an agent
reads this endpoint and does the rest.

Returns component-level health status including database latency, uptime,
and version information. Designed for k8s liveness/readiness probes and
monitoring dashboards. No authentication required.

post.description:
Create a new tenant account and receive an API key. The API key is shown **only once** — store it securely.
Rate limited to 5 signups per hour per IP.

apiKey.description: Your API key — store this securely, not shown again

example.message: Account created. Store your API key securely — it will not be shown again.

post.description:
Retrieve an existing tenant's API key using their email address.
This is a recovery mechanism — treat responses carefully.

404.description: No account found for this email

post.description: Generate a new API key and invalidate the current one. The new key is shown only once.

get.description: Returns a description of the evaluate endpoint and usage instructions. No authentication required.

post.description:
The core evaluation endpoint. Submits an agent action (tool call) to the policy engine
and receives a decision: `allow`, `block`, `monitor`, or `require_approval`.

Authentication is optional — unauthenticated calls use the default policy.
Authenticated calls use tenant-specific custom policies.

sessionId.description: Session identifier for grouping audit events

messageHistory.description: Recent conversation history for context-aware detection

value.reason: Shell execution is blocked by security policy

post.description:
Evaluate up to 50 tool calls in a single request. Each call is evaluated in parallel
against the same effective policy. Individual call failures do not fail the batch.
Returns per-call decisions plus an aggregate summary.

sessionId.description: Session identifier for grouping audit events

results.reason: Shell execution is blocked by security policy
results.suggestion: Use a safer alternative like file_read or file_write

post.description:
Register a new agent and receive a scoped agent API key.
Agent keys (prefixed `ag_agent_`) are scoped to this agent's policy and cannot create tenant accounts.

policyScope.description: Tools this agent is allowed to use

example.name: customer-support-bot
example.note: Store the apiKey securely — it will not be shown again.

get.description: List all active agents for the authenticated tenant.
Retrieve details for a specific agent.

delete.description: Permanently delete an agent and revoke its API key.

post.description:
Dry-run an agent's declared tools through the policy engine to check coverage.
Returns per-tool decisions without logging to the audit trail.
Required before certification.

declaredTools.description: List of tool names the agent will use

post.description:
Mark an agent as certified after achieving 100% policy coverage during validation.
Certified agents appear with a badge in the dashboard.

get.description: Check an agent's current validation coverage and certification status.

ready.description: True if certified and validation coverage is 100%

get.description:
Retrieve paginated audit trail entries for the authenticated tenant.
Entries are chained with SHA-256 hashes for tamper detection.

chainValid.description: Whether the hash chain is valid

get.description:
Retrieve audit events using cursor-based pagination. More efficient than offset-based
pagination for large audit trails. Use the `nextCursor` value from the response as the
`before` parameter in subsequent requests.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor — return events before this ISO 8601 timestamp

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page (null if no more events)

post.description:
Re-compute the hash chain for the tenant's audit trail. Use when hashes are
out of sync due to migration or data import.
Requires admin key.

Register a webhook URL to receive real-time event notifications.
Webhook payloads are HMAC-signed with your optional secret.
Only HTTPS URLs are accepted; private/internal IPs are rejected (SSRF protection).

url.description: HTTPS URL to receive webhook POST requests

secret.description: Optional secret for HMAC signature verification

get.description: List all registered webhooks for the authenticated tenant.

put.description: Update an existing webhook's URL, events, or secret.

get.description: Retrieve the tenant's current effective policy (custom or default).

put.description:
Replace the tenant's custom policy with a new set of rules.
Accepts either a full PolicyDocument or an array of PolicyRule objects.
Maximum 500 rules per policy.

get.description: List all pending human-in-the-loop approval requests for this tenant.

post.description: Approve or reject a pending HITL approval request.

get.description: Retrieve evaluation usage analytics for the past 30 days.

Platform-wide analytics across all tenants.
**Requires admin key.**

Retrieve evaluation usage statistics for the authenticated tenant.
Includes total evaluations, breakdowns by result, last 24h count,
top blocked tools, and average response time.

avgResponseMs.description: Average evaluation response time in milliseconds

post.description: Submit user feedback with a rating (1-5) and optional comment.

comment.example: Great tool, very easy to integrate

agent_id.description: Optional agent this feedback relates to

example.comment: Caught a prompt injection that our tests missed!

get.description:
List all feedback submissions across all tenants.
**Requires admin key.**

post.description:
Anonymous telemetry ping from SDK installations.
No authentication required. Rate limited to 5 per minute per IP.
Always returns 202 (even for invalid payloads) to avoid breaking SDK integrations.

Generate an OWASP Agentic Top 10 compliance report based on the tenant's
audit history and policy configuration. Reports are persisted and retrievable via GET.

agentId.description: Optional — scope report to a specific agent

get.description: Retrieve the most recently generated OWASP compliance report for this tenant.

404.description: No report found — generate one first

get.description: Retrieve a specific OWASP compliance report by its ID.

post.description:
Detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) from text content.
Detects: email addresses, phone numbers, credit card numbers, SSNs, IP addresses,
dates of birth, names, and more.

Raw PII text is **never** stored — only entity type, position, and confidence score.

content.description: Text content to scan for PII

dryRun.description: If true, scan but don't log to audit trail

post.description:
Evaluate a Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool call against the policy engine with
additional SSRF protection — URL arguments are scanned for private network addresses.

ssrfBlocked.description: True if blocked due to SSRF detection

post.description: Register an MCP server with allowed and blocked tool lists for policy enforcement.

allowedTools.description: Tools explicitly allowed from this server

blockedTools.description: Tools explicitly blocked from this server

get.description: List all registered MCP servers for this tenant.

post.description:
Configure Slack HITL (Human-in-the-Loop) integration.
When an evaluation returns `require_approval`, a Slack message is sent
with Approve/Reject buttons. The response is captured via the callback endpoint.

signingSecret.description: Slack app signing secret for HMAC verification

autoRejectMinutes.description: Auto-reject pending approvals after this many minutes

get.description: Retrieve current Slack integration settings. Signing secret is redacted.

delete.description: Remove the Slack HITL integration configuration.

post.description:
Endpoint for receiving Slack interactive component payloads (button clicks).
**Not for direct use** — this is called by Slack when users click Approve/Reject buttons.
Verifies Slack HMAC signature before processing.

Create a child agent with inherited (or more restrictive) policy from the parent.
Policy inheritance rules:
- `blockedTools`: union of parent + child (monotonically restrictive)
- `allowedTools`: intersection of parent + child
- `hitlTools`: union of parent + child
- TTL and maxToolCalls are enforced by the parent's limits

allowedTools.description: Tools this child agent may use (intersected with parent's allowed list)

blockedTools.description: Tools explicitly blocked for this child

hitlTools.description: Tools requiring human approval for this child

maxToolCalls.description: Maximum number of tool calls allowed

get.description: List all child agents spawned by the specified parent agent.

post.description:
Create an in-memory playground session for testing policy evaluation without
full tenant setup. Optionally provide a custom policy for the session.
Sessions are ephemeral — they are not persisted to the database.

policy.description: Optional custom policy document for this session

get.description:
List all available pre-built policy templates. Templates provide curated rule sets
for common compliance frameworks (OWASP, SOC2, EU AI Act, APRA CPS234, Financial Services).
Retrieve a specific policy template by its ID.

200.description: Template details with full rule set

post.description:
Apply a policy template to this tenant's configuration. This records the application
in the audit trail and provides the rules for integration into your policy engine.

get.description: Returns the current state of the global platform kill switch.

post.description:
Activate or deactivate the tenant-level kill switch.
When active, all evaluations for this tenant return `block` immediately.
This is an emergency stop mechanism for incident response.

active.description: Set to true to activate, false to deactivate. Omit to toggle.

example.message: Tenant kill switch ACTIVATED — your evaluations will return BLOCK

post.description:
Pre-flight admission check for an MCP server before connecting it to an agent.
Evaluates each tool the server exposes against the current policy engine and
returns a per-tool admission decision.

serverUrl.description: URL of the MCP server being admitted

description.example: Read a file from the filesystem

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters

admitted.description: True only if all tools are admitted (allowed or monitored)

results.reason: File deletion is blocked by security policy

post.description: Approve a pending human-in-the-loop approval request.
Deny a pending human-in-the-loop approval request.

get.description:
List anomaly-detection alerts for the authenticated tenant.
Optionally filter by severity or resolved state.

post.description: Mark an alert as acknowledged (resolved). Idempotent for already-resolved alerts.

get.description: List custom anomaly detection rules for the authenticated tenant.

post.description: Create a custom anomaly detection rule that generates alerts when triggered.

put.description: Update an existing anomaly detection rule. All fields are optional (partial update).

delete.description: Delete a custom anomaly detection rule.

get.description:
Re-verify the SHA-256 hash chain for the tenant's entire audit trail.
Returns a pass/fail result with error details if tampering is detected.

valid.description: True if the hash chain is intact

errors.description: Details of any chain violations detected

get.description:
Export the full audit trail as CSV or JSON. Supports date range filtering.
For large tenants, use `from`/`to` to paginate by date range.

post.description:
Issue a new API key by verifying email ownership. Invalidates all previous keys.
Rate limited to 2 recovery attempts per hour per IP.
Returns a generic 404 when the email is not found to avoid enumeration.

apiKey.description: New API key — previous key invalidated

post.description:
Activate or deactivate the **global platform-wide** kill switch.
When active, **all tenant evaluations** return `block` immediately.
**Requires admin key.** Use with extreme caution.

active.description: true to activate, false to deactivate

post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout session for upgrading to Pro or Enterprise plan.
Returns a checkout URL — redirect the user to this URL to complete payment.
Requires `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` environment variable to be configured.

cancelUrl.description: Redirect URL if checkout is cancelled

checkoutUrl.description: Stripe Checkout URL — redirect user here

post.description:
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for managing existing subscriptions
(cancel, change plan, update payment method). Returns a portal URL.
Requires an active subscription.

returnUrl.description: URL to return to after leaving the portal

get.description: Returns the current subscription tier, status, and limits for the authenticated tenant.
List all compliance reports generated for the authenticated tenant.

post.description:
Generate a compliance report. Supports multiple report types including OWASP Agentic Top 10.
The generated report is persisted and retrievable by ID.

agentId.description: Optional — scope report to a specific agent

get.description: Retrieve a specific compliance report by its ID.

post.description:
Record an estimated cost event for a tool call. Costs are attributed to
the agent and aggregated for reporting. If `estimatedCostCents` is omitted,
a default cost is inferred from the tool name.

tool.description: Tool name that incurred the cost

estimatedCostCents.description: Estimated cost in cents (USD). Inferred if omitted.

get.description:
Aggregated cost breakdown for the authenticated tenant.
Includes totals by time window, top expensive tools, and daily cost trend.
Cost breakdown grouped by agent for the authenticated tenant.

Aggregated real-time statistics for the tenant dashboard:
evaluation counts (24h/7d/30d), block rate, average latency,
active agent count, top blocked tools, and hourly evaluation breakdown.

blockRatePercent.description: Percentage of evaluations blocked in last 24h

get.description:
Recent evaluation events stream for the live activity feed.
Use `since` for incremental polling — only events newer than that timestamp are returned.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Return only events after this ISO 8601 timestamp (for polling)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return

get.description: Per-agent evaluation activity and cost breakdown for the specified date range (default last 7 days).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of date range (ISO 8601, default 7 days ago)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of date range (ISO 8601, default now)

post.description:
Submit a tool evaluation to be processed asynchronously by the job queue.
Returns a `jobId` and `pollUrl` to check the result. Use when you need
fire-and-forget evaluation without blocking on the response.

toolName.description: Name of the tool to evaluate

pollUrl.description: URL to poll for job status

get.description: Poll a specific async evaluation job for its status and result.
List async evaluation jobs for the authenticated tenant.

post.description:
Issue a new enterprise license key for a tenant.
The raw `AGKEY-...` string is returned **once** — only its SHA-256 hash is stored.
**Requires admin key.**

stripeSubscriptionId.description: Stripe subscription ID for billing linkage

licenseKey.description: Raw license key (AGKEY-...) — shown once only

get.description: Returns the current license tier, feature flags, limits, and this month's usage.

post.description:
Validate a license key against the certificate revocation list (CRL).
Public endpoint — no authentication required.

licenseKey.description: The AGKEY-... license key to validate

post.description:
Revoke a tenant's license key, adding it to the CRL.
The tenant immediately loses access to paid features.
**Requires admin key.**

get.description: Monthly usage history for the last 12 months (evaluations, blocked calls, etc.)

post.description:
Evaluate a tool call within an existing playground session.
Results are stored in the session's in-memory audit log but not persisted.
Authentication: tenant API key or unauthenticated (uses default policy).

get.description: Retrieve the in-memory audit log for a specific playground session.
Returns the effective policy in use for the playground environment.

List pre-built test scenarios for the playground (e.g., prompt injection, SSRF, PII leakage).
No authentication required.

Returns the compiled `PolicyBundle` optimised for client-side local evaluation.
Supports ETag-based conditional requests — clients should send `If-None-Match`
to avoid re-downloading an unchanged bundle. Cached for 60 seconds.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: ETag from a previous response (returns 304 if unchanged)

304.description: Not Modified (ETag matched — bundle is unchanged)

post.description:
Dry-run a list of tool names through the policy engine and return per-tool
coverage decisions. Used to identify gaps before agent certification.

tools.description: Tool names to check coverage for

coveragePercent.description: Percentage of tools with explicit policy rules

covered.description: True if matched an explicit rule (not default)

get.description:
List all saved policy versions for the authenticated tenant (up to the most recent 50).
Use POST /policy/revert/{version} to restore a previous version.

post.summary: Revert policy to a previous version
post.description:
Restore the policy to a previously saved version. The current policy is
automatically saved before reverting (creating a new version).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version number to revert to (from GET /policy/versions)

get.description: Retrieve the GitOps policy sync configuration for the tenant.

put.description:
Configure GitOps policy sync from a GitHub/GitLab repository.
The policy file at `policyPath` is fetched on every sync and replaces
the tenant's custom policy. Use POST /policies/git/sync to trigger a manual sync.

repoUrl.description: HTTPS URL of the Git repository

policyPath.description: Path to the policy YAML/JSON file within the repo

token.description: Personal access token for private repos (stored encrypted)

delete.description: Remove the GitOps policy sync configuration. Policy is NOT deleted.

get.description: List recent Git policy sync events (successes and failures).

post.description:
Manually trigger a policy sync from the configured Git repository.
Fetches the policy file and updates the tenant's active policy.

400.description: Git configuration not found or invalid

post.description: Rollback the policy to a specific Git-synced version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version identifier (commit SHA or version number)

post.description:
Receives GitHub push events to trigger automatic policy sync.
**Not for direct use** — configure this URL as a GitHub webhook.
Verifies the `X-Hub-Signature-256` HMAC before processing.
Triggers a sync when the configured policy file is modified on the target branch.

get.description:
Returns pricing tier information including limits, feature flags, and prices.
Public endpoint — no authentication required. Response is cached for 5 minutes.
List custom per-tenant API rate limit rules.

post.description: Create a custom rate limit rule for a specific endpoint or agent.

endpoint.description: Endpoint path or pattern to rate limit

agentId.description: Scope rule to a specific agent

action.description: Action taken when limit is exceeded

delete.description: Remove a custom rate limit rule.

post.description:
Configure Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) integration for real-time audit
event forwarding. Requires the `siem_export` feature (Pro+ plan).
The HEC token is encrypted at rest.

index.description: Splunk index to write events to

403.description: Feature not available on current plan

get.description: Retrieve current Splunk HEC configuration. Token is masked.

delete.description: Remove the Splunk HEC integration configuration.

post.description: Send a test event to Splunk HEC to verify the configuration.

Configure Azure Sentinel (Log Analytics) integration for real-time audit
event forwarding. Requires the `siem_export` feature (Pro+ plan).
The shared key is encrypted at rest.

sharedKey.description: Log Analytics workspace primary/secondary key (base64, encrypted at rest)

logType.description: Custom log type name in Log Analytics

403.description: Feature not available on current plan

get.description: Retrieve current Azure Sentinel configuration. Shared key is masked.

delete.description: Remove the Azure Sentinel integration configuration.

post.description: Send a test event to Azure Sentinel Log Analytics to verify the configuration.

get.description: Retrieve current SSO (SAML/OIDC) configuration for the tenant. Client secret is masked.

put.description:
Configure SAML 2.0 or OIDC single sign-on for the tenant.
Requires the `sso` feature (Enterprise plan).

ssoUrl.description: SAML SSO URL or OIDC authorization endpoint

403.description: Feature not available on current plan

post.description: Validate the SSO configuration by performing a metadata/discovery check.

get.description:
Initiate an SSO login flow. Redirects to the configured IdP.
For SAML: generates and sends a SAMLRequest. For OIDC: redirects to authorization endpoint.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID to identify the SSO configuration

parameters.name: returnTo
parameters.description: URL to redirect to after successful login

post.description:
OIDC authorization code callback. Processes the `code` and `state` parameters
from the IdP, exchanges the code for tokens, and establishes a session.
**Not for direct use** — this is the redirect target configured in your IdP.

get.description:
SAML 2.0 Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) endpoint — GET variant.
Some IdPs POST the SAMLResponse; this GET variant handles providers that redirect.
**Not for direct use** — configure this as the ACS URL in your SAML IdP.

post.description:
Configure SSO. This is the legacy endpoint — prefer PUT /auth/sso/config.
Both endpoints share the same underlying configuration.

get.description: Get SSO configuration. Legacy alias for GET /auth/sso/config.

delete.description: Remove SSO configuration. Legacy alias for DELETE /auth/sso/config.

get.description:
List all team members for the authenticated tenant.
Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description:
Invite a new team member by email address.
Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

role.description: Role to assign to the new member

delete.description:
Remove a team member from the tenant.
Requires `owner` role.

patch.description:
Update a team member's role.
Requires `owner` role.

get.description: Returns SCIM 2.0 service provider configuration (RFC 7643).
Returns supported SCIM 2.0 schemas (User, Group).

post.description:
Create a SCIM bearer token for use by your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, etc.)
to authenticate SCIM provisioning calls.

get.description: List all active SCIM provisioning tokens for the tenant (tokens are masked).
List users via SCIM 2.0 protocol. Supports `filter`, `startIndex`, and `count`.

post.description: Create a new user via SCIM 2.0 protocol.

get.description: Retrieve a specific user by SCIM ID.

put.description: Replace a user's attributes via SCIM 2.0 PUT (full replacement).

patch.description:
Partial update of a user via SCIM 2.0 PATCH operations (RFC 7644).
Supports `add`, `remove`, and `replace` operations.

get.description: List groups via SCIM 2.0 protocol.

post.description: Create a new group via SCIM 2.0.

get.description: Retrieve a specific group by SCIM ID.

patch.description: Partial update of a group via SCIM 2.0 PATCH operations.

post.description:
Receives Stripe webhook events for subscription lifecycle management.
**Not for direct use** — configure this URL in your Stripe dashboard.
Verifies the `Stripe-Signature` header before processing.
Handles: `checkout.session.completed`, `customer.subscription.updated`,
`customer.subscription.deleted`, `invoice.payment_failed`.
